text: National Scholarship Portal (NSP) General Eligibility: 1. Citizenship: Must be an Indian citizen. 2. Institution: Must be enrolled in a recognized institution. 3. Criteria: Varies significantly by specific scholarship (Pre-Matric, Post-Matric, Merit-cum-Means) based on family income and academic performance. NSP acts as a single window for various central and state government scholarship schemes.

metadata.ministry: Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY) / Various Ministries

text: National Scholarship Portal (NSP) Benefits: 1. Centralized platform for application, tracking, and fund receipt. 2. Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) ensures scholarships are credited directly to the student's bank account. 3. Transparency and reduced administrative burden. 4. Access to multiple scholarship schemes under different ministries (Minority Affairs, Tribal Affairs, etc.) through a single portal.

NSP Application Process: 1. Visit the official portal (https://scholarships.gov.in). 2. Complete the mandatory One-Time Registration (OTR). 3. Login with credentials and select the desired scholarship scheme. 4. Fill the application form and upload documents: Income certificate, Caste certificate, Academic records, etc. 5. Submit and track using the Reference Number.
